title: arctous alpina
intro: Arctous alpina, also known as the bearberry or kinnikinnick, is a low-growing shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America and Europe. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its astringent properties, which are derived from its high concentration of arbutin and ursolic acid. The medicinal properties of Arctous alpina include its ability to treat urinary tract infections, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Arctous alpina is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which are valued for their health benefits, including reducing the risk of kidney stones and alleviating symptoms of arthritis.
